# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 547. Vehicle Equip.
##### § 547.503 — Display of Hazard Lamps

(a) The operator of a vehicle that is described by Subsection (b) and that is stopped on a roadway or shoulder shall immediately display vehicular hazard warning lamps that comply with Section 547.331 (Hazard Lamps Permitted) , unless the vehicle:

    (1) is parked lawfully in an urban district;

    (2) is stopped lawfully to receive or discharge a passenger;

    (3) is stopped to avoid conflict with other traffic;

    (4) is stopped to comply with a direction of a police officer or an official traffic-control device; or

    (5) displays other warning devices as required by Sections 547.504-547.507.

(b) This section applies to a truck, bus, truck-tractor, trailer, semitrailer, or pole trailer at least 80 inches wide or at least 30 feet long.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a

is parked lawfully in an urban district;
is stopped lawfully to receive or discharge a passenger;
is stopped to avoid conflict with other traffic;
is stopped to comply with a direction of a police officer or an official traffic-control device; or
displays other warning devices as required by Sections 547.504-547.507.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: This section applies to a truck, bus, truck-tractor, trailer, semitrailer, or pole trailer at least 80 inches wide or at least 30 feet long.
